name: The Scorched Kingdom
subtitle: Where fire was meant to warm, not consume
theme: Loss / Resolve / The cost of inaction
location: Ember Wastes to Kingdom of Ashveil

shard.name: Seal Fragment of Fire
shard.description: A smoldering red-orange crystal that burns without fuel. A fire that remembers what it used to protect.

scenes.narration: The Kingdom of Ashveil was once the jewel of Aethoria. Now it is a canvas of smoke and silence. Buildings that stood for centuries are rubble.
Nilou kneels beside the remains of a public garden — flowers reduced to ash, a fountain cracked and dry.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: There were people here. Families. There is a child's shoe by that wall.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: ...There is no one left to guide them across. That bothers me more than it should.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: This is what happens when darkness is left unopposed. I have seen what fear-driven choices cost people.

scenes.narration: An old keeper, burned and barely standing, watches them with hollow eyes.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Old Keeper: You are not shadow-soldiers. You are real. I have not seen that in weeks.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: The demon lord who did this — where is he?

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Old Keeper: The throne room. He has the red crystal — says it feeds him, that he will use it to call the Emperor here.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: Calling the Emperor through a Fragment. Clever. In a catastrophically evil way.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: Then we take it from him first.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The path to the throne room cuts through ruins patrolled by shadow-corrupted beasts and undead soldiers.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
pre_dialogue.text: They were soldiers once. Now they are instruments. Do not hesitate.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
pre_dialogue.text: No sentimentality until after the fight. Then we can be sad properly.

post_dialogue.speaker: Nilou
post_dialogue.text: I keep thinking about those people who lived here. That is why we cannot stop.

post_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
post_dialogue.text: Hold onto that. It is a better reason to fight than duty alone.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: People who lose everything make the best companions. You stop taking things for granted.

post_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
post_dialogue.text: Move. The demon lord will not wait.

chapters.title: The Cinder Passage

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The direct path to the throne room is collapsed — floors caved in, walls crumbled to slag. There is only one way through: the old cinder passage beneath the city.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
pre_dialogue.text: The underground tunnels. The old keeper mentioned them. They connect beneath the palace.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
pre_dialogue.text: Crawling through the underbelly of a ruined kingdom? I have done worse before breakfast. Lead on!

pre_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
pre_dialogue.text: The passage will not be empty. Stay together. Stay sharp.

chapters.map_hint: Navigate the cinder passage — reach the palace entrance on the other side.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: The passage opens into the palace undercroft. Scorched columns rise around them, and at the far end — a set of great doors, sealed with shadow fire.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: Look at that. We made it through without getting horribly lost. I am genuinely impressed with us.

post_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
post_dialogue.text: The throne room is beyond those doors. He knows we are here.

post_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
post_dialogue.text: Then we do not make him wait.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The Demon Lord sits on Ashveil's stolen throne, the fire Seal Fragment pulsing against his chest like a second heartbeat. The heat in the room is not natural. It presses against skin like a hand that has not decided whether to push or hold.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: Demon Lord: Summoned warriors. How quaint. Valdris will be amused that you died before reaching him.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
pre_dialogue.text: Ohoho~! Bold last words for someone about to learn what a funeral director can really do!

pre_dialogue.speaker: Nilou
pre_dialogue.text: That Fragment does not belong to you. It never did. Give it back to the world you took it from.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
pre_dialogue.text: We have faced worse. Much worse. This ends now.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: The Demon Lord falls. Not with a roar — with a sound like an exhale. Like something holding its breath for a very long time finally letting go.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: The red crystal floats free from the ash. Its burning light shifts — from predatory to something older. Warmer. Like a hearthfire that remembers what homes felt like.

post_dialogue.speaker: Nilou
post_dialogue.text: It is changing. The moment he lost hold of it — it changed completely.

post_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
post_dialogue.text: The corruption was in the wielder, not the Fragment itself. It was never evil. Just captured.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: Two down. Three to go. I think I am starting to genuinely enjoy this quest.

post_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
post_dialogue.text: You are not taking it seriously enough.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: I am taking it seriously AND enjoying it. Multitasking is a core funeral director skill.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: Then a presence settles over the throne room. Not a sound. Not a figure. A weight — the kind that has no temperature, that presses from every direction at once.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: Valdris: You freed the Fragment. Good. That creature was wasting it.

post_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
post_dialogue.text: You are watching us.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: Valdris: I have always been watching. You are the most interesting thing to enter my world in a century. The Oracle chose well — four resonances, cleanly matched. I almost respect it.

post_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
post_dialogue.text: The kingdom. The people. This destruction — this is your doing. Not the Demon Lord's. Yours.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: Valdris: I broke a Seal. The unraveling was a consequence. I do not govern consequences. That is what mortality is — an endless chain of consequences that someone else pays.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: There was a child's shoe in that garden.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: Valdris: I have consumed worlds older than that child's entire civilization. Grief is a luxury I shed six centuries ago. Come. Shatter my anchors. Break everything I have built. I will be patient.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: Valdris: I am very good at patient.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: The presence lifts. The ash settles. The four stand in the silence of a kingdom that will never fully recover — and carry the second Fragment forward anyway.

post_dialogue.speaker: Nilou
post_dialogue.text: He is not afraid of us at all.

post_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
post_dialogue.text: No.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: Good thing we are not doing this to frighten him.

post_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
post_dialogue.text: Forward. That is all there is now.
